county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: O'Brien County formed 1851 from Indian lands. County seat is Primghar.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Primghar holds deeds from 1860; Clerk of District Court has probate records. O'Brien County was settled in the 1870s-1880s by English, Irish, and Scandinavian immigrants. Primghar's name was formed from the initials of eight pioneer citizens. O'Brien County Historical Society. Sheldon, Sanborn, and Paullina communities. Little Sioux and Ocheyedan Rivers. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to O'Brien County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: O'Brien County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Primghar
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1851 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: O'Brien County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Primghar
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1851. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: O'Brien County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Primghar / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1851. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: O'Brien County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for O'Brien County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: O'Brien County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for O'Brien County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: O'Brien County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / O'Brien County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for O'Brien County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: O'Brien County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / O'Brien County Auditor, Primghar
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for O'Brien County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: O'Brien County Scandinavian Church Records -- Lutheran Congregations
source_profiles.repository: Luther College Archives, Decorah / ELCA Archives / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Norwegian and Swedish Lutheran church records for O'Brien County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, burials. Many Norwegian Synod and Hauge Synod congregations.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Osceola County formed 1851 from Indian lands. County seat is Sibley.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Sibley holds deeds from 1872; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Osceola County in Iowa's extreme northwest was settled late (1870s-1880s). Dutch and German immigrants dominate early records. Ashton, Melvin, and Ocheyedan communities. Ocheyedan Mound (highest point in area). Osceola County Historical Society. Dutch Reformed and German Lutheran church records. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Osceola County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Page County formed 1847 from Fremont. County seat is Clarinda.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Clarinda holds deeds from 1852; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Page County was settled by migrants from the upper South and Midwest. Clarinda was the birthplace of Glenn Miller. Clarinda State Hospital (Mental Health Institute, est. 1885) institutional records at State Archives. Shenandoah's radio history -- KMA and KFNF were pioneering radio stations. Page County Historical Society. Nodaway River valley. College Springs (Amity College). FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Page County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1847. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1847. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Palo Alto County formed 1851 from Indian lands. County seat is Emmetsburg.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Emmetsburg holds deeds from 1858; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Palo Alto County was settled in the 1870s-1880s. Emmetsburg was an Irish Catholic colony, one of the strongest in Iowa, established under Bishop John Ireland's colonization program. Catholic parish records are essential. Palo Alto County Historical Society. West Bend (with the Grotto of the Redemption, a religious shrine). Ruthven and Ayrshire communities. Des Moines River headwaters. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Palo Alto County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Plymouth County formed 1851 from Indian lands (Woodbury area). County seat is Le Mars.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Le Mars holds deeds from 1870; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Plymouth County was the center of the 'British Colony' -- English aristocratic settlers established cattle ranches in the 1870s-1880s (the 'Close Colony'). Le Mars is the 'Ice Cream Capital of the World' (Wells Enterprises). Plymouth County Historical Society. Kingsley and Remsen communities. Big Sioux River valley. Floyd River. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Plymouth County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1851. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Pocahontas County formed 1851 from Humboldt, Greene. County seat is Pocahontas.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Pocahontas holds deeds from 1860; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Pocahontas County was settled in the 1870s-1880s after railroad development. Pocahontas and Laurens communities. German and Scandinavian settlers. Lizard Creek and Des Moines River. Pocahontas County Historical Society. Fonda, Havelock, and Rolfe communities. Palmer and Gilmore City. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Pocahontas County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Polk County formed 1846 from Indian lands. County seat is Des Moines.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Des Moines holds deeds from 1846; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Polk County is Iowa's most populous county, home to Des Moines, the state capital (since 1857). State Historical Society of Iowa headquarters in Des Moines holds statewide collections. Iowa State Archives in Des Moines is the primary repository for state government records. Iowa Genealogical Society (IGS) in Des Moines is the largest genealogical society in the state. Polk County Historical Society. Des Moines Public Library has strong local history. Diverse immigrant communities: Italian (south side), Greek, African American, and Southeast Asian. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Polk County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Polk County communities. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage announcements, land sale notices. State Historical Society of Iowa holds microfilm of many Iowa newspapers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Pottawattamie County formed 1847 from Indian lands (Council Bluffs area). County seat is Council Bluffs.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Council Bluffs holds deeds from 1849; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Pottawattamie County is centered on Council Bluffs, a major Missouri River crossing and gateway to the West. Mormon Trail headquarters at Kanesville (renamed Council Bluffs) -- Brigham Young was sustained as church president here in 1847. LDS Church records for this period are at Church History Library, SLC. Union Pacific Railroad terminus -- railroad records. Pottawattamie County Historical Society. Dodge House Museum (General Grenville Dodge). FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Pottawattamie County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Council Bluffs
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1847. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Council Bluffs / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1847. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.repository: LDS Church History Library, Salt Lake City / State Historical Society of Iowa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Latter-day Saint emigrant records, pioneer journals, and camp records for the Pottawattamie County area of the Mormon Trail across Iowa (1846-1869). Includes both LDS members and non-Mormon settlers who arrived along the same corridor.

source_profiles.repository: State Historical Society of Iowa / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Pottawattamie County communities. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage announcements, land sale notices. State Historical Society of Iowa holds microfilm of many Iowa newspapers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Poweshiek County formed 1843 from Iowa, Keokuk. County seat is Montezuma.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Montezuma holds deeds from 1848; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Poweshiek County was named for the Meskwaki (Fox) chief Poweshiek. Grinnell College (est. 1846) in Grinnell has archives and a strong abolitionist heritage. J.B. Grinnell was an abolitionist leader. Poweshiek County Historical Society. Montezuma and Brooklyn communities. Deep River and Malcom. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Poweshiek County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Poweshiek County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Montezuma
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1843 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Poweshiek County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Montezuma
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1843. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Poweshiek County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Montezuma / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1843. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Poweshiek County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Poweshiek County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Poweshiek County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1850-1950 for Poweshiek County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Poweshiek County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Poweshiek County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Poweshiek County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Poweshiek County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Poweshiek County Auditor, Montezuma
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Poweshiek County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Ringgold County formed 1847 from Taylor. County seat is Mount Ayr.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Mount Ayr holds deeds from 1855; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Ringgold County in southern Iowa was settled by migrants from Missouri, Indiana, and Ohio. Mount Ayr was an agricultural market town. Ringgold County Historical Society. Kellerton, Benton, and Diagonal communities. Grand River and Thompson River valleys. Mormon Trail corridor (southern route). FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Ringgold County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Mount Ayr
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1847. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1847. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Ringgold County Auditor, Mount Ayr
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Ringgold County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Mormon Trail Records -- Ringgold County Area
source_profiles.repository: LDS Church History Library, Salt Lake City / State Historical Society of Iowa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Latter-day Saint emigrant records, pioneer journals, and camp records for the Ringgold County area of the Mormon Trail across Iowa (1846-1869). Includes both LDS members and non-Mormon settlers who arrived along the same corridor.
